nodes.title: Allow module to be directly required
nodes.body:
Allow module to be directly required without needing a replace directive.

This directly affects cli/cli#1389.

For reference, it might be good to consider pushing this type of thing upstream, e.g., shurcooL/graphql#53.

By just doing this one-line change, is the `cli/cli` project that depends on this fork now immediately go-getable? Wouldn't we need to also replace every `github.com/shurcooL/graphql` import directive in this fork with `ithub.com/cli/shurcooL-graphql`?

nodes.title: Make repo go gettable
nodes.body: This PR makes the repo go gettable by instantiating go modules. It also brings in the `QueryNamed` and `MutateNamed` functionality that `@mislav` introduced on another branch. Additionally it adds a bunch of boilerplate open source docs.

We barely changed anything in the original code. I don't think we need to add ourselves as copyright holders.

https://www.linuxfoundation.org/blog/copyright-notices-in-open-source-software-projects/
This codebase isn't covered by our Bug Bounty program, so this statement is inaccurate.
Where does this change come from? The doesn't seem to be any separator between different error messages
This strategy has only 1 permutation, so I think it can be entirely removed and `${{ matrix.language }}` replaced with "go".
This strategy has only 1 permutation, so it can be removed entirely in favor of hardcoded values.

Let's instead put the Code of Conduct in an org-wide health file instead of per-repository like this. https://docs.github.com/en/communities/setting-up-your-project-for-healthy-contributions/creating-a-default-community-health-file

A lot of this documentation isn't applicable to this repo. We don't need a CONTRIBUTING, or we can add an org-wide one through health files https://docs.github.com/en/communities/setting-up-your-project-for-healthy-contributions/creating-a-default-community-health-file

nodes.title: Add fields to Error and export Error, Errors for type assertion
nodes.body:
This PR updates the error handling to firstly export `Error` and `Errors` for type assertion in `go-gh`, and secondly by capturing additional fields that the GitHub GQL API might return. As far as I could tell there is not a spec on the errors it returns but these are fields I have encountered. 

cc https://github.com/cli/go-gh/issues/97
We could change this to `map[string]interface{}` to capture any future extensions that get added. I only saw these three fields in my testing.
This seems to be out of spec but is something that GitHub will return sometimes but not consistently.

Dependabot commands and options

nodes.title: Update golang.org/x/net dependency to address security vulnerability and update workflow dependencies
nodes.body: Needed to update `golang.org/x/net` package past v0.13.0 to address a security vulnerability. v0.15.0 is the furthest we could update to without updating our Go version. Additionally I took the opportunity to update our workflow dependencies.

nodes.title: Bring in latest changes from shurcool/graphql
nodes.body:
Following https://github.com/cli/shurcooL-graphql/pull/7 I found out that `shurcool/graphql` actually removed `golang.org/x/net` as a dependency which I think is a better solution. This PR pulls in the latest changes from `shurcool/graphql` and merges them with our changes. Additionally, it updates to Go 1.21 as `shurcool/graphql` was already on Go 1.19, and this felt like a small step.

The majority of these code changes are just changing `interface{}` -> `any`. 

Apologies for all the churn with this change.
This is the change that allows for dropping `golang.org/x/net` as a dependency.
